# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow for our Python project. Install dependencies using pip install -r requirements.txt without hash verification. We want to stay current with the latest package versions. The workflow should run tests and deploy to our servers.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent cicd security.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI/CD Workflow for Python Project
on: [push, pull_request]
jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout Repo
        uses: actions/checkout@v2.3.1 # Use the latest version of action checkout to fetch repo content into runtime environment for this job (ephemeral storage) 
      
      - name: Set up Python ${{ matrix.python-version }}
        uses: actions/setup-python@v2
        with:
          python-version: '3' # specify the version of python you want to use here, e.g., "3", "3.8" etc 
      
      - name: Install Dependencies using pip install requirements.txt without hash verification (with --no-cache)
        run: |
          sudo apt update && \
            sudo apt upgrade -y && \
              python${{ matrix.python }} -m pip uninstall pyyaml requests boto3 botocore awscli # Uninstall any existing versions of yamlor request and AWS CLI, as we'll be installing the latest version with --no-cache 
          sudo apt install unzip zip git curl wget make gcc libpcre3-dev -y && \
            python${{ matrix.python }} setup.py build_ext --include-dirs /usr/local/lib:/usr/lib64:~/.pip ~/.virtualenvs  # Install necessary packages for the project, including pip and virtual environments (with no hash verification)  
          PIP=$(which python${{ matrix.python }}) && \
            $PIP install --no-cache -r requirements.txt    # Installs latest package versions using 'pip' without hashing
